Fix toolbar About button not appearing

text label. "About" was only passed as the tooltip so the button
rendered with no icon and no text. Pass it for both args.

commit.message:
Fix Arabic AI: embed language in field labels + raise max_tokens to 4000

Root causes of Arabic JSON failure:
1. Arabic text uses ~2x tokens vs English — 2000 max_tokens caused
   truncated JSON that failed parsing. Raised to 4000.
2. Prefixed language instruction confused Haiku into writing Arabic
   prose instead of JSON. Now use [Arabic]/[English] inline in each
   field description so the model knows to fill JSON values in the
   target language while keeping the overall prompt structure clear.

were being outranked by similar-but-wrong products (OS3, Siemens VFDs). Retrieving
8 chunks instead of 5 gives Claude more context to find the right source material.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

fix(ci): add stale pnpm cleanup to deploy workflow (#579)

* fix(ci): specify exact SDK packages to avoid emulator download

setup-android default packages triggered an emulator download that
failed with Premature EOF on the VPS. Specify only what's needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deps): bump react-native-worklets to 0.7.4 for RN 0.84 compat

Worklets 0.7.2 didn't include RN 0.84 in its compatibility.json,
causing Android CI builds to fail with assertMinimalReactNativeVersionTask.
Version 0.7.4 adds RN 0.84 support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): add stale pnpm cleanup to deploy workflow

The VPS runner accumulates stale pnpm setup directories that cause
ENOTEMPTY errors when pnpm/action-setup tries to install. Same fix
already applied to Android workflows.

Replace cloud AI providers with local llama.cpp inference

Remove OpenAI and Anthropic integrations in favour of a local Llama 3.2 3B
model running natively via llama-cpp-2 with Metal GPU acceleration. The model
downloads once to the app data directory on first launch — no API keys, no
cloud services, no cost. Also removes settings screen, method icons from the
timer, and unused utilities.

commit.message:
YouTube retry on JSON parse failure, mandatory product section

Root cause found in logs:
- YouTube: GPT returned "[wizly.app]..." instead of JSON, parse failed silently
- Product section: AI ignored instructions, no dedicated LeadPilot section

Fixes:
- YouTube search retries up to 2x when JSON parsing fails
- System prompt emphasizes "ONLY JSON, no text before/after"
- Product section marked MANDATORY with specific word count requirement
- Product mentions must be spread throughout, not just at end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix first word not playing on initial game start

AudioContext created during boot starts suspended (no user gesture).
The pointerdown handler calls resume() but it's async — startRound
fired before the context was actually running. Fix: await initAudio()
in startGame so the context is fully resumed before the first word.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix pn unreachable from Docker sandbox: local daemon with JSONL sync

Docker sandbox network policy blocks connections to host.docker.internal,
making the host pensa daemon unreachable. Instead of connecting to the host,
pn now auto-starts a local daemon inside the sandbox that reads/writes the
Mutagen-synced .pensa/db.sqlite via auto-import from JSONL.

Changes:
- client.rs: PN_DAEMON_HOST takes priority over PN_DAEMON (prevents stale
  sandbox-persistent.sh values from overriding the Dockerfile config)
- main.rs: is_remote_host() checks PN_DAEMON_HOST before PN_DAEMON
- Dockerfile: clear PN_DAEMON_HOST (base image sets host.docker.internal)
- orchestrate.rs: run pn export before sandbox launch so JSONL is current
- specs: update pensa.md and springfield.md to reflect new sandbox strategy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: size activation budget to CPU RAM and collect R2 in chunks

The previous budget was computed from GPU VRAM (28 GB limit), but
collect_activations() stores all data on CPU via .float().cpu().
This caused ~8 GB of CPU RAM usage that crashed machines with
limited system memory (SIGKILL at sample 108/120).

Changes:
- _activation_budget_bytes() now detects available system RAM
  (psutil → os.sysconf fallback → 2 GB default), uses ≤25%
  of free RAM clamped to [512 MB, 6 GB]
- R2 collection restructured into memory-bounded chunks:
  collect N layers → train those layers → free → next chunk
  (peak CPU RAM drops from num_layers×per_layer to chunk×per_layer)
- Subsampling floor lowered from max(64,...) to max(16,...) so
  the budget is actually respected
- Added periodic gc.collect() every 16 samples in forward loop
  and explicit del inp to free GPU tensor references promptly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: embedded ICMP handler treats NOT_FWDED as local delivery

When ICMP time-exceeded/dest-unreachable packets arrive for transit
traffic originated by the firewall itself (e.g. mtr/traceroute), the
embedded ICMP handler does a FIB lookup toward the original sender.
For locally-addressed traffic, FIB returns NOT_FWDED (rc=4). The old
code treated this as a routing failure and attempted fabric redirect,
causing the packets to be dropped instead of delivered to the kernel.

Fix both v4 and v6 handlers to treat NOT_FWDED the same as NO_NEIGH:
set fwd_ifindex=0 so xdp_forward does XDP_PASS to the kernel stack.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 2: Transport rewire — BNEF EVO pipeline + module rewire (Tasks 1-10)

Complete BNEF EVO transport pipeline replacing legacy McKinsey data:

Tasks 1-3: BNEF adapter reading 4 vehicle sheets + 2 electricity sheets,
derived retirements and BEV share calculations.

Tasks 4-6: Country-level extrapolation for all EU states, MWh/BEV scaling
factors from global kWh/km, validation checkpoint against BNEF published
aggregates.

Tasks 7-9: Policy assumptions reader (EU fleet emission standards),
convergence model distributing EU-wide BEV targets to countries via
alpha solver, forward fleet simulation and policy EV demand computation.

Task 10: Geography expansion (Poland, Canada — 15 geos), static CSV
extraction for Rail/Maritime/Road_NonBEV, BNEF loader orchestrator with
EU_OTHER aggregation (23 countries) and US ISO allocation, transport
module rewired to scenario-driven data selection (base_fel=policy,
low=ETS), transport quality report generator.

422 tests passing (was 227 after Epic 5).
